children.label: Britain
children.notes: see also "United Kingdom"

children.label: Burma
children.notes: see also "Myanmar"

children.label: Myanmar
children.notes: see also "Burma"

children.label: United Kingdom
children.notes: see also "Britain"

children.label: Class
children.notes: refers to social and socioeconomic class as a category of analysis

children.label: Low income
children.notes: refers to low-income people / populations or issues that impact or foreground lower-income people, populations, or class status

children.label: Middle-income / middle class
children.notes: refers to middle-income people / populations or issues that impact or foreground middle-income people, populations, or class status

children.label: High-income / upper class
children.notes: refers to high-income people / populations or issues that impact or foreground high-income people, populations, or upper-class status

children.label: Population density
children.notes: refers to population density as a category of analysis

children.label: Urban
children.notes: refers to high population density in urban areas

children.label: Suburban
children.notes: refers to medium population density in suburban areas

children.label: Rural
children.notes: refers to low population density in rural areas

children.label: Sex and gender
children.notes: refers to sex and gender as categories of analysis

children.label: Man
children.notes: refers to people who identify as a man or issues that focus specifically on men as group / study population

children.label: Woman
children.notes: refers to people who identify as a woman or issues that focus specifically on men as group / study population

children.label: Transgender
children.notes: refers to people who currently identify or previously identified as transgender or transitioning or issues that foreground or centrally impact trans people / populations

children.label: Intersex
children.notes: refers to people who identify or are identified as intersex (an individual who does not fit into predetermined biological sex categories)

children.label: Educational level
children.notes: refers to education level as a general category of analysis (for issues of educational structures as limiting or sites of oppression, see “op”)

children.label: Primary education
children.notes: refers to people who have limited formal education; in the U.S., a primary (elementary school) education or to issues focused on this population

children.label: Secondary education
children.notes: refers to people who have (at least) some high school education, or who have earned a high school diploma or its equivalent (e.g., in the U.S., high school but no advanced work) or to issues focused on this population

children.label: Higher education
children.notes: refers to people who have (at least) some college education or have earned a college degree or to issues focused on this population

children.label: Age
children.notes: refers to age as a category of analysis

children.label: Child
children.notes: refers to children, roughly ages 0-11

children.label: Teen
children.notes: refers to teens and pre-teens, roughly ages 11-18

children.label: Young adult
children.notes: refers to young adults, roughly ages 18-25

children.label: Senior
children.notes: refers to older adults, roughly 65 and above

children.label: Relationship / marital status
children.notes: refers to relationship / marital status as a category of analysis (see also “Polygamy / polyamory” for polygamous marriage)

children.label: Married
children.notes: refers to people in legal marriages

children.label: Divorced
children.notes: refers to people who are legally divorced

children.label: Separated
children.notes: refers to people who are separated, either legally or non-legally

children.label: Cohabitating
children.notes: refers to people living with a significant other in a domestic partnership but not legally married

children.label: Single
children.notes: refers to people not married or cohabitating with a significant other

children.label: Widows and widowers
children.notes: refers to people who have outlived their spouse

children.label: Parent / parenting
children.notes: refers to people who identify as parents and issues related to parenting

children.label: Immigration as legal and / or social status
Immigrant
children.notes: refers to people who have left their country of origin and relocated elsewhere, not including refugees or asylum seeker

children.label: Alien or non-resident
children.notes: refers to temporary and long-term legal non-citizen immigrants (U.S.)

children.label: Undocumented immigrant
children.notes: refers to immigrants without legal status

children.label: Refugee
children.notes: refers to people who have left their community of origin as a result of persecution, war, or conflict

children.label: Asylum seeker
children.notes: refers to people who seek legal asylum from a country other than their country of origin

children.label: Minor
children.notes: refers to people below the age of 18

children.label: People who qualify for government assistance
children.notes: refers to people who qualify for government support, including medicare, medicaid, welfare etc., or who access universal healthcare

children.label: Convicted criminal
children.notes: refers to anyone who has been convicted of a crime in a court of law

children.label: Incarcerated
children.notes: refers to people who are or have been under correctional control, including residents of a prison (incarcerated people may not be convicted criminals and vice versa, e.g. someone incarcerated before conviction)

children.label: Sex offender
children.notes: refers to people legally registered as sex offenders or required to do so by rule / statute or issues that foreground the regulation and treatment of this population

children.label: People impacted by social and environmental conflict and displacement
Natural disaster
children.notes: refers to people who have lived through natural disasters or issues that foreground the impact of natural disasters

children.label: War and conflict
children.notes: refers to people living in areas of war and conflict who have remained in their country of origin and have not sought refuge elsewhere or issues that foreground the impact of war / conflict

children.label: Pandemic
children.notes: refers to people affected by pandemic and its aftermath or issues that foreground the impact of pandemic

children.label: Homelessness
children.notes: refers to people who lack permanent shelter and / or research about issues of homelessness

children.label: Race and ethnicity
children.notes: refers to racial and / or ethnic background as a category of analysis (typically in an American context)

children.label: Black / African American
children.notes: refers to people who identify as black and / or African-American

children.label: Hispanic
children.notes: refers to people who identify as Hispanic

children.label: Latino/a/e/x
children.notes: refers to people who identify as Latino / Latina / Latine / Latinx

children.label: Southasian
children.notes: refers to people who identify as South Asian or Indian

children.label: Asian / Asian American
children.notes: refers to people who identify as Asian and / or Asian-American

children.label: White / Caucasian
children.notes: refers to people who identify as white or Caucasian

children.label: Indigenous populations
children.notes: refers to people who identify as members of an indigenous population, including Native Americans / American Indians, Native Canadians, and Aboriginal Australians

children.label: Middle Eastern
children.notes: refers to people who identify as Middle Eastern and / or Arab

children.label: Religion and religious affiliation
children.notes: refers to members of a religious group, including religious officials or research that focuses its analysis on one or more religion, religious belonging, or religious community; also applies to articles that refer to survivors' relationship to faith, spirituality, or religion = religious (see also "faith")

children.label: Christian
children.notes: refers to people who identify as Christian or research that centers Christian faith, doctrine, belonging, or community

children.label: Catholic
children.notes: refers to people who identify as Catholic or research that foregrounds questions of Catholic faith or community

children.label: Muslim
children.notes: refers to people who identify as Muslim or research that foregrounds questions of Islamic faith, doctrine, or community

children.label: Jewish
children.notes: refers to people who identify as Jewish or research that foregrounds questions of Jewish faith, doctrine, or community

children.label: Hindu
children.notes: refers to people who identify as Hindu or research that foregrounds questions of Hindu faith, doctrine, or community

children.label: Buddhist
children.notes: refers to people who identify as Buddhist or research that foregrounds questions of Buddhist faith, doctrine, or community

children.label: Confucian
children.notes: refers to people who identify as Confucian or research that foregrounds questions of Confucian faith, doctrine, or community

children.label: Occupational and institutional roles and vulnerabilities (for victimization and perpetration)
College student, staff, or faculty
children.notes: refers to students, staff, or faculty of colleges or universities

children.label: Social worker
children.notes: refers to people in professional or paraprofessional roles that support and / or advocate for vulnerable populations

children.label: Government
children.notes: refers to people who are employed by the government

children.label: Military personnel
children.notes: refers to members of the armed forces and / or their intimate partners

children.label: Police / law enforcement personnel
children.notes: refers to members of law enforcement agencies (i.e. police) and / or their intimate partners

children.label: Domestic worker and caregiver
children.notes: refers to domestic workers and caregivers, including in-home nurses and child care workers

children.label: Sex worker
children.notes: refers to people who sell sex and other sexual services including prostitutes and sex workers

children.label: Athletes
children.notes: refers to members of athletic teams, amateur or professional, and associated issues

children.label: Fraternities
children.notes: refers to members of a (typically male) collegiate social club and related issues

children.label: Sororities
children.notes: refers to members of a female collegiate social club and related issues

children.label: Gang
children.notes: refers to street gangs and other similar group / organizations

children.label: Heterosexual / straight
children.notes: refers to those who engage in hetersexual practices

children.label: Polygamy / polyamory
children.notes: refers to people who engage in multiple sexual relationships with the awareness of all involved parties (for polygamous marriage, also see “marital”)

children.label: Risky sex
children.notes: refers to people who engage in sexual practices characterized as risky by researchers, such as unprotected or substance-facilitated sex

children.label: BD / SM
children.notes: refers to those who engage in bondage, discipline / sadism, masochism as well as more general sexual practices of domination and consensual force

children.label: Inter-generational
children.notes: refers to those who engage in sexual activity with an individual from a different generation

children.label: Queer sexual identities and practices
children.notes: refers to queer sexual identities and practices

children.label: Gay
children.notes: refers to people who identify as gay

children.label: Lesbian
children.notes: refers to people who identify as lesbian

children.label: Bisexual
children.notes: refers to people who identify as bisexual and / or have sex with people of the same and (an)other gender(s)

children.label: Asexual
children.notes: refers to people who identify as asexual and / or aromantic, or the sexual practice that does not include a desire for intercourse or other sexual activity

children.label: Men who have sex with men
children.notes: refers to men who have sex with other men, regardless of whether or not they identify as other than straight

children.label: Women who have sex with women
children.notes: refers to women who have sex with women, regardless of whether or not they identify as other than straight

children.label: People with illness
children.notes: refers to people with an illness or disease, including chronic pain

children.label: People with physical disability
children.notes: refers to people with physical disabilities including but not limited to blindness, deafness, cerebral palsy (for “ableism,” see under “System of oppression”)

children.label: People with cognitive impairment
children.notes: refers to people with cognitive disabilities such as down syndrome and autism

children.label: People with mental illness
children.notes: refers to people with mental illnesses, including mood disorders and psychotic disorders

children.label: People with trauma, including PTSD
children.notes: refers to people who have experienced trauma and / or experience Post Traumatic Stress Disorder

children.label: People with eating disorder
children.notes: refers to people who have or have had eating disorder and research that investigates eating disorders / disordered eating and its impacts

children.label: People who self-harms
children.notes: refers to people who have harmed or currently harm their own body physically and research that investigates self harming practices

children.label: People with HIV / AIDS
children.notes: refers to people who have been diagnosed with HIV / AIDS and / or affected by HIV / AIDS and related research

children.label: People with STIs (not including HIV / AIDS)
children.notes: refers to people who have been diagnosed with (a) Sexually Transmitted Infection(s) excluding HIV / AIDS and related research

children.label: People who abuse drugs
children.notes: refers to people who abuse drugs and research investigating drug abuse / dependency

children.label: People who abuse alcohol
children.notes: refers to people who abuse alcohol and research investigating alcohol abuse / dependence

children.label: Pregnant people / pregnancy
children.notes: refers to people who are or have recently been pregnant and research that foregrounds pregnancy and pregnant people

children.label: Perpetrator
children.notes: refers to perpetrators of abuse or violence of any kind

children.label: Bystander
children.notes: refers to people who are bystanders in violence, whether or not they took action to prevent, diffuse, or deflect violence, as well as programs aimed at training bystanders to take such action

children.label: Intimate partner violence
children.notes: refers to relationship violence that is physical, sexual and / or emotional (for non-consensual sexual acts between people who know each other including intimate partner, see also “acquaintance” under "Sexual Violence")

children.label: Domestic violence
children.notes: (in this context) refers to relationship violence excluding sexual violence

children.label: Intimate partner murder
children.notes: refers to homicide committed by a perpetrator against a current or former intimate partner

children.label: Battered persons who kill
children.notes: refers to homicide committed by victims / survivors of abuse against their current or former intimate partner

children.label: Stalking
children.notes: refers to repeated and unwanted contact directed at a specific person that causes fear or emotional distress (see also “socialmedia” for social media being a tool for surveillance / stalking)

children.label: Cyber stalking and other electronic aggressions
children.notes: refers to stalking, abuse, and / or harassment, in which technology and / or social media plays a significant role, including sharing sexually explicit material without consent

children.label: Child abduction
children.notes: refers to abduction of children

children.label: Child abuse (not including child sexual abuse)
children.notes: refers to physical or emotional abuse committed against children

children.label: Child sexual abuse
children.notes: refers to sexual abuse perpetrated against children

children.label: Child custody issue
children.notes: refers to custody and visitation issues

children.label: Child exposure to abuse
children.notes: refers to children's exposure to abuse perpetrated against a parent or guardian

children.label: Child neglect
children.notes: refers to non-sexual abuse perpetrated against children, excluding cases labeled by researchers as corporal punishment (see "corporal punishment")

children.label: Spanking and corporal punishment
children.notes: refers to corporal punishment / disciplinary violence of children, such as spanking, excluding cases labeled by researchers as physical abuse (see "Child abuse")

children.label: Separation from parents / guardian
children.notes: refers to children who have lost or been separated from their original parents / guardians as a result of death, war, natural disaster, or legal custody battles

children.label: Trafficking & prostitution
children.notes: refers to sex trafficking, sexual slavery, and prostitution

children.label: Incest
children.notes: refers to sexual violence perpetrated by a family member of the victim / survivor, including both intergenerational and intragenerational incest

children.label: Non-acquaintance rape
children.notes: refer to non-consensual sex (most often penetrative) perpetrated by someone the victim / survivor does not know

children.label: Acquaintance rape
children.notes: refers to non-consensual sexual acts between people who know each other (for sexual assault between strangers, see “rape”)

children.label: Sexual assault
children.notes: refers to any attempted or completed non-consensual sexual act

children.label: Gang rape
children.notes: refers to sexual assault perpetrated by a group of people against an individual

children.label: Drug and alcohol facilitated sexual violence
children.notes: refers to sexual violence that is facilitated using drugs and / or alcohol

children.label: Sexual violence in facilities, including detention, prisons, and healthcare centers
children.notes: refers to sexual violence in facilities and institutions, including detention centers, prisons, and health care centers

children.label: Harassment
children.notes: refers to harassment generally

children.label: Workplace harassment
children.notes: refers to Violence and / or harassment perpetrated in a place where people work

children.label: K-12 bully / harassment in schools
children.notes: refers to violence and harassment in K-12 schools, by and against students

children.label: Coercion and / or non-physical violence
children.notes: refers to non-physical violence, including types of coercion involving implied or explicitly conveyed threats and forceful words / actions, such as reproductive coercion

children.label: Emotional abuse / control
children.notes: refers to emotional abuse, including blaming, shaming, and degrading comments

children.label: Economic abuse / control
children.notes: refers to economic abuse and control, including control and monitoring of an abused person's finances

children.label: Issue of consent
children.notes: refers to issues of consent, including defining consent and the "consent defense"

children.label: Witnessing violence
children.notes: refers to exposure to violent acts, including acts of war, terrorism, and gun violence, not include child exposure to domestic violence perpetrated by a member of the household (see “exposure” under Children)

children.label: Structural violence
children.notes: refers to violence built into social and institutional structures that causes people distress, pain, or suffering and / or prevents them from accessing systems and resources to which they have a right

children.label: Political system
children.notes: refers to structural violence in political systems, including voter suppression practices

children.label: Legal system
children.notes: refers to structural violence in legal systems, including marginalizing laws and barriers to access to legal services

children.label: Workplace policies and system
children.notes: refers to structural violence in workplace policies and systems, including barriers to access to employment or abortion / birth control through employer health insurance

children.label: Healthcare system
children.notes: refers to structural violence in health care policies and systems, including barriers to access to abortion and / or birth control

children.label: Educational system
children.notes: refers to structural violence in educational systems, including barriers to education for women or low income people

children.label: Religious or ritualized structure
children.notes: refers to structural violence in religious or ritualized beliefs, practices, and organizations

children.label: Female genital cutting and circumcision
children.notes: refers to female genital mutilation, female genital cutting, and / or female circumcision

children.label: Systems of oppression
children.notes: refers to socially and culturally constructed ideologies and patterns of behavior that oppress historically and institutionally disempowered groups

children.label: Racism
children.notes: refers to negative attitudes, stereotypes, violence and / or discriminatory action based on ethnicity / race (including white supremacy)

children.label: Sexism
children.notes: refers to negative attitudes, stereotypes, violence and / or discriminatory action based on sex / gender

children.label: Heteronormativity
children.notes: refers to interventions and responses that reinforce that structural primacy and normalcy of heterosexuality in society

children.label: Classism
children.notes: refers to negative attitudes, stereotypes, violence and / or discriminatory action based on class

children.label: Xenophobia
children.notes: refers to negative attitudes, stereotypes, violence and / or discriminatory action toward immigrants or foreigners

children.label: Colonialism
children.notes: refers to policy or practice of acquiring or exerting full or part political control over another country

children.label: Ableism
children.notes: refers to discriminatory practices that favor able-bodied people

children.label: Homophobia
children.notes: refers to negative attitudes, stereotypes, violence and / or discriminatory action based on sexual orientation, including both polysexualities and monosexualities

children.label: Transphobia
children.notes: refers to negative attitudes, stereotypes, violence and / or discriminatory action based on trans, gender variant, or non-binary gender identity

children.label: Antisemitism
children.notes: refers to negative attitudes, stereotypes, violence and / or discriminatory action toward Jews

children.label: Islamophobia
children.notes: refers to negative attitudes, stereotypes, violence and / or discriminatory action toward Muslims

children.label: Suicide
children.notes: refers to attempted or completed, and / or suicidal ideation

children.label: Use of weapons
children.notes: refers to the use of weapons, including knives, blunt objects, guns etc.

children.label: General criminal activity
children.notes: refers to general criminal activity

children.label: Non-sexual violence
children.notes: may or may not be committed alongside sexual violence

children.label: Murder
children.notes: refers to homicide committed by a perpetrator against anyone other than a current or former intimate partner

children.label: Physical violence
children.notes: refers to physical violence committed by a perpetrator against anyone other than a current or former intimate partner (see also "Corporal punishment" under Children for physical violence committed against minors).

children.label: Animal abuse
children.notes: refers to physical abuse of animals, often committed alongside spousal abuse

children.label: Gender-based violence (including "femicide")
children.notes: refers to violence committed specifically on the basis of gender, including honor killings and femicide

children.label: Lynching
children.notes: refers to racial terrorism via public, premeditated, extrajudicial killing

children.label: Forced labor
children.notes: refers to all types of coerced work that an individual must do against their will, including prison labor

children.label: Chattel slavery
children.notes: refers to forced labor wherein race is constructed to create a class of laborers who are under the complete domination of owners who have powers of life and death over them, can sell and transfer them at will, and has full control over their daily and domestic life including their offspring

children.label: Indentured servitude
children.notes: refers to a mutual agreement wherein a laborer is compelled to work in order to repay a debt (contract conditions may be illegal and / or vastly more beneficial to the employer than the laborer)

children.label: Masculinity
children.notes: refers to a social construct affecting men, boys, and / or male behavior

children.label: Rape myths
children.notes: refers to myths about rape and other forms of sexual assault, upheld by cultural norms and sometimes social structures

children.label: Revictimization
children.notes: refers to multiple victimizations of one person by the same or a different perpetrator over the course of the victim / survivor’s lifetime

children.label: Retraumatization
children.notes: refers to multiple experiences of trauma over a lifetime, often completed by governmental, legal, and / or healthcare systems

children.label: Recidivism
children.notes: refers to repeated perpetration and punishments

children.label: Cycle of violence
children.notes: refers to cycle of violence in which people who are abused, especially as children, become abusers themselves

children.label: Intergenerational trauma
children.notes: refers to intergenerational trauma caused by oppression

children.label: Stigma
children.notes: refers to the shame or disgrace felt or assigned to a characteristic or circumstance

children.label: Guilt
children.notes: refers to internalized feelings of self-blame or shame discussed either as a risk factor for victimization or as a result of having witnessed and / or experienced abuse or assault

children.label: Memory
children.notes: refers to the memory of victims / survivors or witnesses of sexual assault or intimate partner violence

children.label: Genetic determinants
children.notes: refers to genetic determinants and / or hereditary links as an influential factor in the perpetration of or exposure to gender violence

children.label: Media and visual culture
children.notes: refers to representations of violence in media and visual culture

children.label: Social media
children.notes: refers to representations of violence, rhetoric that enforces violence, and resistance against violence on social media platforms (for scenarios that social media is the tool for abuse like , see also “cyber” under "Stalking")

children.label: Television
children.notes: refers to representations of violence on television

children.label: Violence against and by celebrities
children.notes: refers to violence perpetrated by and / or against celebrities

children.label: News media
children.notes: refers to representations of violence in news media

children.label: Pornography
children.notes: refers to representations of violence in pornography, the effects of pornography, and / or its underlying messages

children.label: Fiction
children.notes: refers to representations of violence in non-scholarly fictional literature

children.label: Non-fiction
children.notes: refers to representations of violence in non-scholarly nonfictional literature

children.label: Comic books
children.notes: refers to representations of violence in comic books

children.label: Film
children.notes: refers to representations of violence in film

children.label: Music
children.notes: refers to representations of violence in music and music videos

children.label: Video games
children.notes: refers to representations of violence in video games

children.label: Stand-up comedy
children.notes: refers to representations of violence in stand-up comedy

children.label: Theater
children.notes: refers to reprentations of violence in theatrical productions

children.label: International governing bodies and NGOs
children.notes: refers to international responses from governing bodies like the UN and international non-governmental organizations (NGOs)

children.label: Judicial proceedings
children.notes: refers to any and all judicial proceedings in response to structural or interpersonal violence

children.label: College judiciary
children.notes: refers to judicial proceedings for sexual assault carried out by a college judiciary board or similar governing body

children.label: Domestic violence and family courts
children.notes: refers to responses from courts specifically designed to handle issues of domestic violence and / or child custody

children.label: Orders of protection
children.notes: refers to orders of protection, also known as restraining orders and protective orders, and issues related to their access and enforcement

children.label: Criminal proceedings
children.notes: refers to judicial proceedings involving one or more criminal charge

children.label: Custody rulings
children.notes: refers to judicial rulings related to the custody of children and dependents

children.label: Supervised visitation
children.notes: refers to court rulings and regulations for supervised visitation of children

children.label: Restorative justice
children.notes: refers to judicial system that prioritizes victim / survivor agency, the role of the community, harm reparation through reconciliation, and rehabilitation instead of punishment

children.label: Expert testimony in trial proceedings
children.notes: refers to advice for prosecutors concerning expert testimony in trial proceedings

children.label: Law enforcement
children.notes: refers to action taken by law enforcement agencies and / or employees, including police and / or prosecutors

children.label: Mandatory arrest
children.notes: refers to policies regarding mandatory arrest in cases involving domestic violence

children.label: Intra-state
children.notes: refers to law enforcement policies of a state or international equivalent

children.label: Inter-state and / or federal
children.notes: refers to law enforcement policies of the federal government, as well as any other inter-state legal cooperation, or international equivalent

children.label: Sex offender registries
children.notes: refers to state or federal registries for convicted sex offenders

children.label: Policy and legislation
children.notes: refers to policies and legislation as responses and associated issues

children.label: National
children.notes: refers to policies and legislation on a national level

children.label: State
children.notes: refers to policies and legislation on a state level or international equivalent

children.label: Municipal
children.notes: refers to policies and legislation in a specific district or municipality, such as a city, school district, or Indian reservation

children.label: Education, training, and intervention programs
children.notes: refers to programs for education, training, and / or rehabilitation in response to specific and / or structural violence (see “bystander” for bystander intervention)

children.label: Batterer intervention
children.notes: refers to Programs for perpetrators aimed at reducing their risk of recidivism, i.e. becoming repeat offenders

children.label: Group programs
children.notes: refers to group intervention programs for perpetrators

children.label: Individual counseling
children.notes: refers to individual intervention programs for perpetrators

children.label: Male consciousness raising
children.notes: refers to consciousness-raising among men involving discussion of masculinity, including fathering groups and boys' groups

children.label: School and campus education programs
children.notes: refers to programs in K-12 or primary and secondary schools schools and on college campuses to educate students, faculty, and staff

children.label: Workplace education programs
children.notes: refers to educational workplace programs, including diversity training and anti-harassment programs

children.label: Intervention by / for medical professionals
children.notes: refers to interventions enacted by and for medical professionals

children.label: Public health outreach programs
children.notes: refers to outreach programs based on a public health model / framework

children.label: Trauma-informed approaches and interventions
children.notes: refers to explicitly trauma-informed approaches and interventions

children.label: Community initiatives
children.notes: refers to initiatives developed by communities rather than a legal or governmental body

children.label: Grassroots activism
children.notes: refers to non-hierarchical, community-based activism and aid systems

children.label: Community notification
children.notes: refers to community notification of the presence of sex offenders or other violence in the community

children.label: Safety planning
children.notes: refers to creation of a plan for the safety of victims / survivors, including options for income, shelter, and legal redress

children.label: Risk factors and assessment
children.notes: refers to risk factors and assessment thereof

children.label: Shelters for IPV survivors
children.notes: refers to shelters for victims / survivors of intimate partner violence (see also “homeless”)

children.label: Crisis hotlines
children.notes: refers to hotlines for victims / survivors of sexual and / or intimate partner violence

children.label: Centers that offer walk-in resources
children.notes: refers to centers that offer walk-in resources, including health screenings

children.label: SART, SANE, ER and other team responses
children.notes: refers to Sexual Assault Response Teams (SART), Sexual Assault Nurse Examiners (SANE), and ER professionals attending to victims / survivors of sexual or intimate partner violence in hospital settings

children.label: Faith-based initiatives
children.notes: refers to initiatives implemented by religious organizations and / or according to faith-based goals and / or values

children.label: Survivor's social supports
children.notes: refers to victim / survivor's social support system, including friends, family and coworkers

children.label: Volunteer management and preparation
children.notes: refers to strategies for volunteer management and preparation

children.label: Coordinated community response
children.notes: refers to a set of strategies to combat gender violence on the community level that involves cooperation between multiple institutions

children.label: Community advocacy
children.notes: refers to advocacy programs created or run on the community level

children.label: Court
children.notes: refers to advocacy programs within domestic violence courts and / or family courts

children.label: Survivor-centered
children.notes: refers to advocacy programs and / or response systems that prioritize the needs of the victim / survivor

children.label: Family-centered
children.notes: refers to advocacy and response systems designed to keep the family intact

children.label: Child-centered
children.notes: refers to advocacy and response systems that prioritize the needs of the child

children.label: Psychiatric treatment
children.notes: refers to medical treatment for mental illness and associated issues

children.label: Individual therapy
children.notes: refers to individual therapy for mental illness and associated issues, including counseling

children.label: Couples therapy
children.notes: refers to couples therapy concerning intimate partner violence in relationships

children.label: Family therapy
children.notes: refers to family therapy concerning domestic violence

children.label: Third party mediation
children.notes: refers to solution-focused, third-party intervention in an interpersonal dispute (see also “collegiate”)

children.label: Group therapy
children.notes: refers to group therapy for victims / survivors, including counseling

children.label: Regulations for and obligations of professionals employed by the state
children.notes: refers to regulations for and obligations of professionals employed by the state

children.label: Regulations for social workers
children.notes: refers to regulations for and obligations of social workers regarding mandatory reporting

children.label: Regulations for teachers
children.notes: refers to regulations for and obligations of teachers regarding mandatory reporting

children.label: Self reporting
children.notes: refers to self-reporting or disclosure by victims / survivors and / or perpetrators

children.label: Mandatory reporting
children.notes: refers to the legal obligation to report suspected abuse, i.e. mandatory reporting

children.label: Coping mechanism
children.notes: refers to behaviors and actions, healthy or unhealthy, intended to reduce stress and / or distress, including self-care practices

children.label: Coping (family)
children.notes: refers to coping mechanisms on the family level

children.label: Coping (individual)
children.notes: refers to coping mechanisms on the individual level

children.label: Self-defense / resistance
children.notes: refers to self-defense, including formal training and / or informal actions taken by individuals to avoid or resist violence

children.label: Bidirectional intimate partner violence
children.notes: refers to articles specifically describing intimate partner violence that is bidirectional

children.label: Resilience
children.notes: refers to resilience as the characteristic of victims / survivors to overcome trauma and adversity, including post-traumatic growth

children.label: Scholarly article in a peer-reviewed journal or publication
children.notes: refers to an article that appears in a peer-reviewed scholarly journal

children.label: Non-scholarly article
children.notes: refers to any item that is not a scholarly article, whether or not it appears in a peer-reviewed journal

children.label: Law review article
children.notes: refers to a scholarly article that appears in a law review

children.label: Studies on the economic effects of abuse on society
children.notes: refers to source that concerns the macroeconomic effects of gender violence in society

children.label: Statistical
children.notes: refers to source that is primarily statistical in nature, rather than an article containing statistics

children.label: Profile
children.notes: refers to source that develops a profile for rapists, abusers, batterers, survivors / victims, etc.

children.label: Autobiographical (memoir / personal narrative)
children.notes: refers to source that is autobiographical in nature, such as a memoir or an individual story, told by one or more victims / survivors, field practitioners, or researchers

children.label: Anecdotal report by practitioner
children.notes: refers to source that consists of or includes anecdotal report(s) by one or more field practitioners

children.label: Needs assessment
children.notes: refers to needs assessment of a given institution or population

children.label: Handbook or practitioner guide
children.notes: refers to handbook or practitioner guide for workers in the field of gender violence

children.label: Film review
children.notes: refers to a review of a video, film or documentary

children.label: Documentary film
children.notes: refers to documentary film or miniseries, i.e. nonfiction media based in fact

children.label: Book review
children.notes: refers to a review of a published book

children.label: Poem
children.notes: refers to poem or review of a poem

children.label: Critique
children.notes: refers to critique and / or review of scholarship and developments in specific reference to a previous study

children.label: Respond to a critique of a previous study
children.notes: refers to source that specifically responds to a critique of a previous study

children.label: U.S. government
children.notes: refers to study or source funded by the US government

children.label: Non-profit / NGO
children.notes: refers to study or source sponsored by an NGO

children.label: Sponsored by an agency of a sovereign nation
children.notes: refers to study or source funded by a government of a sovereign nation other than the US, excluding the UN

children.label: Social science article
children.notes: refers to study that takes a social science approach, including adherence to the scientific method

children.label: Systematic review of statistical data
children.notes: refers to study that performs a statistical analysis of all the data available on a given topic, i.e. a meta-analysis

children.label: Meta studies
children.notes: refers to source that provides an overview of past research and scholarly developments on a given topic (see also “sys”)

children.label: Randomized control trial
children.notes: refers to blind or double-blind study using randomized selection of participants, experimental conditions, and a control group for comparison

children.label: Experimental study
children.notes: refers to study that explicitly identifies its design as experimental, usually including a control group

children.label: Single group design
children.notes: refers to study that explicitly identifies its design as experimental but does not include a control group

children.label: Quasi-experimental design
children.notes: refers to study with a quasi-experimental design lacking randomized selection of participants

children.label: Longitudinal study
children.notes: refers to study with a research design involving repeated observations of the same variables over a period of time

children.label: Qualitative study
children.notes: refers to study that uses primarily qualitative methods

children.label: Exploratory study
children.notes: refers to exploratory study designed to provide the foundation for further in-depth research

children.label: Design / methodology as a category of analysis
children.notes: refers to sources that discuss research design and / or methodology as a category of analysis

children.label: Ethics of research as a category of analysis
children.notes: refers to sources that discuss the ethics of research as a category of analysis
